channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: NGO Job Opportunity: Programme Officer (PWD and Refugees) at Forum for African Women Educationalists – Nairobi
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Forum for African Women Educationalists (FAWE) is seeking an experienced Programme Officer – Persons with Disabilities (PWD) and Refugees to support its mission of advancing gender equity and equality in education across Africa. The successful candidate will play a key role in strengthening inclusive education programmes that empower African girls and young women, particularly […]

The post NGO Job Opportunity: Programme Officer (PWD and Refugees) at Forum for African Women Educationalists – Nairobi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Forum for African Women Educationalists (FAWE) is seeking an experienced Programme Officer – Persons with Disabilities (PWD) and Refugees to support its mission of advancing gender equity and equality in education across Africa. The successful candidate will play a key role in strengthening inclusive education programmes that empower African girls and young women, particularly those living with disabilities and those in refugee communities.

This position offers an opportunity to contribute to transformative education initiatives across multiple African countries while working with a dynamic pan-African organization committed to inclusive development.

About FAWE

The Forum for African Women Educationalists (FAWE) is a pan-African non-governmental organization operating through a network of 34 National Chapters across the continent. FAWE works to advance gender equality in education by influencing policy, fostering positive societal attitudes, and promoting inclusive practices that ensure girls and women can access and thrive in education systems.

Position Overview

The Programme Officer – PWD and Refugees will provide leadership and technical expertise in advancing social inclusion within FAWE programmes, as outlined in the FAWE Strategic Plan (2024–2028) and the organizational work plan.

The role focuses on ensuring girls and young women with disabilities and refugee backgrounds have access to quality education and skills training opportunities that prepare them for the world of work.

Job Details

  • Position: Programme Officer – PWD and Refugees

  • Organization: Forum for African Women Educationalists

  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya (Regional Office)

  • Contract Type: Fixed-term contract (3 years), with possible extension based on performance and funding availability

  • Reporting To: Senior Programme Officer

  • Travel: Frequent travel across the region

The role will collaborate closely with FAWE teams, National Chapters, civil society organizations, and regional policy bodies.

Key Result Areas

The Programme Officer will lead initiatives that strengthen the inclusion of persons with disabilities and refugees within FAWE’s education programmes.

Core focus areas include:

  • Strategic leadership on social inclusion initiatives

  • Programme research, evaluation, and sustainability planning

  • Resource mobilization for inclusive education programmes

Key Roles and Responsibilities

1. Programme Management (60%)

  • Lead social inclusion initiatives within FAWE programmes across 10 African countries:

    • Uganda

    • Ethiopia

    • Rwanda

    • Zambia

    • Malawi

    • Zimbabwe

    • Tanzania

    • Senegal

    • Liberia

    • Ghana

  • Promote innovative strategies that integrate gender equity, transformative education, and social inclusion.

  • Ensure strong quality assurance systems for programme implementation.

  • Support partnerships and programme expansion initiatives.

2. Capacity Building and Programme Development (20%)

  • Conduct internal assessments to identify capacity-building needs related to social inclusion.

  • Support FAWE National Chapters in strengthening inclusive education programming.

  • Build alliances with organizations working with persons with disabilities and refugee communities.

3. Partnership Building and Advocacy (10%)

  • Strengthen partnerships with project partners, national chapters, and regional stakeholders.

  • Advocate for increased civic space and education opportunities for refugee girls and girls with disabilities.

  • Collaborate with FAWE advocacy and partnerships teams to amplify marginalized voices.

4. Resource Mobilization (10%)

  • Identify funding opportunities for social inclusion initiatives.

  • Provide technical input in grant proposals and funding pitches.

  • Participate in engagement and negotiations with development partners.

Qualifications and Experience

Applicants should possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Social Sciences such as:

    • Education

    • Gender and Development

    • Public Health

    • Political Science

    • Philosophy

    • Law

    • Project Management

    • Or a related field

  • Minimum 7 years of relevant professional experience, including at least 3 years in the NGO sector.

  • Experience managing multi-country partnerships and consortiums.

  • Strong skills in report writing, programme documentation, and presentations.

  • Knowledge of education and social inclusion issues.

Bilingual proficiency in English and French is highly desirable.

Key Competencies

Ideal candidates should demonstrate:

  • Strategic thinking and innovative programme design

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

  • Excellent communication and writing skills in English

  • Strong networking and stakeholder engagement abilities

  • Effective planning and organizational skills

  • Integrity, professionalism, and teamwork

  • Flexibility and willingness to travel internationally

Diversity and Inclusion

The Forum for African Women Educationalists strongly encourages applications from qualified individuals from diverse backgrounds.

Applications from people living with disabilities are particularly encouraged as FAWE is committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable workplace where all individuals are respected and valued.

Application Procedure

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and FAWE may fill the position before the deadline if a suitable candidate is identified early. Interested applicants are therefore encouraged to apply as soon as possible.

How to Apply

  1. Complete the official application form via the Microsoft Forms link:
    2. Submit the fully completed form before the deadline.

Application Deadline:
11:59 PM on Wednesday, 13 March 2026

If you are passionate about inclusive education, gender equality, and empowering marginalized girls across Africa, this role offers an opportunity to contribute to meaningful change at a continental level.

Discover more Job Opportunities by clicking here. 

The post NGO Job Opportunity: Programme Officer (PWD and Refugees) at Forum for African Women Educationalists – Nairobi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 for African Women Scientists (Africa–Australia Partnership)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
African women scientists working in agriculture and climate research can now apply for the Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026, a prestigious leadership and career development program focused on climate-resilient agriculture and gender-responsive agrifood systems. The fellowship is implemented by African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) under the Africa–Australia Partnership for Climate […]

The post Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 for African Women Scientists (Africa–Australia Partnership) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African women scientists working in agriculture and climate research can now apply for the Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026, a prestigious leadership and career development program focused on climate-resilient agriculture and gender-responsive agrifood systems.

The fellowship is implemented by African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) under the Africa–Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture and aims to strengthen the leadership capacity of women researchers driving climate adaptation and sustainable agriculture across Africa.

Key Details

Fellowship: Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship
Organizer: African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD)
Program Duration: 24 months
Eligible Countries: Egypt, Ghana, Morocco, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone
Target Participants: African women scientists in agriculture and climate research
Degree Requirement: Minimum Master’s degree in a relevant discipline
Experience Required: At least 7 years of professional experience
Language Requirement: English or French proficiency
Application Fee: None (Free to apply)
Application Deadline: April 26, 2026 (23:59 East Africa Time – GMT+3)

About the Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship

The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is designed to strengthen the capability, confidence, and influence of African women scientists working in agriculture and climate research.

Through leadership development, mentoring partnerships, and technical learning opportunities, the fellowship prepares women researchers to become leaders in climate-resilient agriculture and agrifood systems innovation.

The program draws inspiration from the successful AWARD Fellowship Model, connecting participants with a broader ecosystem of agricultural researchers, mentors, and leaders.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will participate in a comprehensive professional development program that includes:

  • Leadership and negotiation training

  • Mentoring partnerships with both mentors and mentees

  • Training on gender integration in climate adaptation

  • Science communication and publishing courses

  • Networking with agricultural and climate research experts

  • Collaboration with AWARD alumni and partner networks

The fellowship will engage up to 300 beneficiaries, including:

  • 100 Fellows

  • 100 Mentors

  • 100 Mentees

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, applicants must:

  • Be African women scientists working in agriculture, climate science, or agrifood systems

  • Have a minimum of seven years of professional experience in agriculture, climate research, gender integration, or related fields

  • Hold at least a master’s degree in a relevant discipline such as:

    • Agriculture

    • Agricultural Economics

    • Agricultural Extension

    • Natural Resource Management

    • Food Science and Nutrition

    • Climate Science

  • Be nationals of the target countries and residing in Africa

  • Demonstrate strong interest in advancing gender-responsive and climate-resilient research

  • Be proficient in English or French

Applicants must also show the potential to influence research, policy, and innovation in agrifood systems.

Program Approach

The program adopts a two-track implementation approach:

Fellowship Track

This track focuses on career development for women scientists, providing mentorship, leadership training, and professional growth opportunities.

Institutional Strengthening Track

This component supports national agricultural research systems (NARS) in integrating gender-responsive approaches into institutional policies and research programs for equitable climate action.

Target Participants

Participants will be drawn from institutions across the agricultural research ecosystem, including:

  • National Agricultural Research Institutions (NARIs)

  • Ministries of Agriculture

  • Universities and academic institutions

  • International agricultural research organizations

  • Climate and agrifood research centers

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through the official fellowship application portal.

Applicants should apply through the appropriate portal depending on their language preference:

  • English-speaking applicants: Apply through the English application portal  here 

  • French-speaking applicants: Apply through the French application portal here 

Applicants living with disabilities who require reasonable adjustments during the application process can contact:

please email T.Wangaya@cifor-icraf.org .

There is no application or registration fee.

📅 Application Deadline: April 26, 2026 (23:59 East Africa Time – GMT+3)

Late applications will not be accepted.

For more information, please download and read:

Fellowship Brochure 

Frequently Asked Questions

Interested candidates are invited to apply by April 26, 2026, at 23:59 East Africa Time (GMT+3). Applications will NOT be accepted after this date. 

Apply Now

About the Africa–Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture

The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is funded through the Africa–Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture.

The partnership aims to strengthen agricultural research capacity and increase the number of women leading climate-resilient agriculture initiatives across Africa.

The program is delivered by the Australian Centre for International Agricultural Research (ACIAR) in collaboration with the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT).

Why This Fellowship Matters

Women scientists are critical to transforming Africa’s agricultural systems, climate resilience strategies, and food security efforts.

By investing in leadership development and research capacity, the fellowship helps ensure that women researchers play a central role in shaping sustainable, inclusive, and climate-resilient agrifood systems across Africa.

Acknowledgement 

The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is an initiative of African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) funded under the Africa—Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture. It seeks to contribute to the Partnership’s end-of-program outcome two (EOPO2): “Australia leverages its funding to national and multilateral agricultural research institutes to increase the number of women leading and participating in climate resilient agriculture”. The Africa—Australia Partnership is delivered by the Australian Centre for International Agricultural Research (ACIAR), in collaboration with the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. Source https://awardfellowships.org/news/call-to-apply-for-the-women-in-agriculture-leadership-program-fellowship-for-the-africa-australia-partnership-for-climate-responsive-agriculture/ 

The post Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 for African Women Scientists (Africa–Australia Partnership) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Nonprofit organizations working to support children and teenagers can now apply for the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program, funded by the Cigna Group Foundation. The foundation has committed $9 million over three years to support programs that improve youth mental health, emotional well-being, and access to mental health care for young people aged 5–18. […]

The post $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Nonprofit organizations working to support children and teenagers can now apply for the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program, funded by the Cigna Group Foundation.

The foundation has committed $9 million over three years to support programs that improve youth mental health, emotional well-being, and access to mental health care for young people aged 5–18.

This funding opportunity focuses on addressing the growing mental health challenges affecting children and adolescents, particularly post-pandemic stress, anxiety, and emotional distress.

Organizations working with parents, caregivers, educators, therapists, and youth-serving professionals are also encouraged to apply.

📅 Application Deadline: March 12, 2026 (5:00 PM ET)

About the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program

The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program supports nonprofit organizations delivering evidence-informed programs that help children and teens build resilience, develop social-emotional skills, and access mental health services.

The initiative responds to the growing need for early intervention and mental health support among young people.

Even before the COVID-19 pandemic, one in five children aged 3–17 experienced a mental, emotional, developmental, or behavioral disorder. Since the pandemic, the need for effective mental health support systems for youth has increased significantly.

The Cigna Foundation aims to partner with organizations that can deliver scalable solutions, strengthen community support systems, and expand access to care for underserved youth populations.

Grant Focus Areas

Nonprofits applying for funding must align their programs with at least one of the following priority goals:

1. Strengthening Social-Emotional Skills

Programs that help youth develop skills related to:

  • Emotional regulation

  • Stress management

  • Resilience

  • Mental well-being

2. Supporting Parents, Caregivers, and Youth Professionals

Initiatives that equip adults with the tools and knowledge to support youth mental health, including:

  • Educators

  • Youth workers

  • Therapists

  • Parents and guardians

3. Expanding Access to Mental Health Care

Programs that improve pathways to mental health intervention, including:

  • Early identification of mental health challenges

  • Access to counseling or therapy

  • School-based mental health services

  • Community mental health programs

Examples of Funded Youth Mental Health Programs

The Cigna Foundation has previously supported a wide range of impactful initiatives through this grant program.

2024 Partner Projects

Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2024 nonprofit partners include:

  • A holistic, trauma-informed after-school program designed to empower teen boys and girls severely impacted by challenging life circumstances.
  • Building social-emotional learning skills in high-school students and educating parents, caregivers, and educators about specific mental health challenges of the students.
  • Subsidized therapy for youth, as well as mental health training for local youth-serving organizations and schools.
  • Explore outcomes from this cohort in our Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report.

Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2025 nonprofit partners include:

  • A virtual program led by medical students that helps youth in grades 6-12 build skills to manage their stress, develop an understanding of how to take care of their own mental health, and respond to peers experiencing suicidal thoughts.
  • A Students Supporting Students program that recruits and trains peers, supported by school staff, to identify signs and symptoms of mental health issues and empowers them to lead a mental health campaign in their school.
  • Cost-free, equitable access to school-based care, within the school setting and during school hours, using personalized treatment plans.

Who Should Apply

This grant opportunity is designed for nonprofit organizations that serve children and adolescents experiencing mental health challenges.

Ideal applicants include organizations that:

  • Provide youth mental health services

  • Deliver school-based mental health programs

  • Support social-emotional learning

  • Train parents, caregivers, and educators

  • Offer early intervention or therapy services for youth

  • Work with underserved or high-need communities

Programs should be evidence-informed and focused on measurable outcomes for youth well-being.

Why This Grant Matters

Investing in youth mental health today helps build healthier, more resilient communities tomorrow.

The Cigna Foundation believes that supporting the mental well-being of young people strengthens families, communities, and society as a whole.

Through partnerships with nonprofits, the foundation aims to expand access to care, strengthen support networks, and ensure that children and teens have the resources they need to thrive.

How to Apply

Organizations interested in applying for funding can submit their applications before the deadline.

The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant application is open now until March 12, 2026 at 5 pm ET.

Download the Request for Application (RFA) [PDF] for details about the grant program.

Register and apply now

Learn More

Read our frequently asked questions.

Related Links

  • The Cigna Group Foundation

Related Resources

  • Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report
  • 2025 Youth Mental Health Press Release
  • 2024 Youth Mental Health Press Release

1 Protecting Youth Mental Health, Department of Health and Human Services, 2021, https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/surgeon-general-youth-mental-health-advisory.pdf

Source https://www.thecignagroup.com/our-impact/esg/healthy-society/community/foundation/improving-youth-mental-health

The post $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Public health professionals across Africa and the African diaspora can now apply for the Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program – Strategy Development Expert role under the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC). This prestigious opportunity deploys experienced experts to support national public health systems, strategic planning, and health policy development across Africa. The programme contributes […]

The post Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Public health professionals across Africa and the African diaspora can now apply for the Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program – Strategy Development Expert role under the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC).

This prestigious opportunity deploys experienced experts to support national public health systems, strategic planning, and health policy development across Africa.

The programme contributes to Africa’s Health Security and Sovereignty agenda, strengthening the continent’s ability to prevent, detect, and respond to public health threats.

Key Details

Organization: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC)
Programme: Kofi Annan Scholars Program – African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC)
Role: Strategy Development Expert
Number of Positions: 20
Deployment Type: On-call expert roster
Contract Type: Volunteer roster with paid deployments
Location: Deployments across African Union Member States
Application Deadline: 2 April 2026 (11:59 PM EAT)

Successful applicants will join a roster of Strategy Development Experts who may be deployed to support Ministries of Health, National Public Health Institutes (NPHIs), and public health institutions across Africa.

About the Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program

The Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program (KA-GHLP) was launched by the African Union Commission and Africa CDC in partnership with the Kofi Annan Foundation.

The initiative aims to build a new generation of African public health leaders capable of strengthening health systems and improving pandemic preparedness across the continent.

The program includes three main components:

• Public Health Leadership Fellowship
• Public Health Scholars Program
• Public Health Policy Forum

The Kofi Annan Scholars Program specifically deploys experienced professionals into time-bound technical assignments to strengthen national health systems.

Role of the Strategy Development Expert

Strategy Development Experts will support governments and public health institutions in designing and implementing evidence-based health strategies and institutional frameworks.

These assignments may involve developing:

• National public health workforce strategies
• Institutional development plans
• Health system strengthening frameworks
• Emergency preparedness and response strategies

Experts will also ensure alignment with Africa CDC’s New Public Health Order and national priorities.

Key Responsibilities

Selected experts will:

• Conduct situational analyses and capability assessments
• Identify policy and operational gaps in public health systems
• Draft national strategies and institutional plans
• Facilitate strategic planning consultations and stakeholder workshops
• Develop implementation roadmaps and monitoring frameworks
• Produce technical reports, policy briefs, and planning guidance
• Support resource mobilization and partner coordination
• Design monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) systems

Eligibility Requirements

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must hold a:

• Master’s or PhD in Public Health
• Health Policy
• Health Systems Management
• Strategic Planning or related field

Professional Experience

Applicants should have:

• At least 7 years of experience in strategic planning or public health policy
• Experience working with Ministries of Health, NPHIs, or development partners
• Demonstrated ability to develop national strategies or institutional frameworks
• Knowledge of African health systems and policy environments

Experience with Africa CDC initiatives or African Union frameworks such as Agenda 2063 is an advantage.

Required Skills

Technical Skills

• Policy analysis and strategic planning
• Institutional development and system strengthening
• Stakeholder engagement and facilitation
• Strategic writing and policy communication

Personal Competencies

• Adaptability in complex environments
• Strong cultural awareness and emotional intelligence
• Ability to work independently and in teams
• Commitment to service and impact

Language Requirements

Applicants must be proficient in at least one African Union working language:

• English
• French
• Arabic
• Portuguese
• Spanish
• Swahili

Knowledge of multiple AU languages is an advantage.

Benefits of Joining the AVoHC Roster

Although membership in the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) does not provide payment during the roster period, experts receive benefits during deployments.

During Deployment

• Round-trip travel tickets
• Health insurance
• Stipend and allowances
• Accommodation allowance (when applicable)
• Communications and incidentals support
• Certificate of recognition

Additional Opportunities

• Professional development training
• Mentorship opportunities
• Networking with experts from 55 African Union member states
• Participation in high-impact public health initiatives across Africa

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

• Completed African Union CV Template
• Updated CV
• Copy of African passport
• Academic certificates and qualifications

Only fully completed applications will be considered.

Application Deadline

2 April 2026, 11:59 PM EAT

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Gender Equality and Inclusion

Africa CDC is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply.

Candidates from underrepresented African Union member states are also encouraged to apply.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official African Union recruitment portal:

APPLY HERE 

 

FOR MORE DETAILS PLEASE VISIT 

The post Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar & Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
PhD researchers working on European migration and asylum law have a unique opportunity to present their research, receive expert feedback, and potentially receive financial support through the 2026 Odysseus Summer School Doctoral Seminar. Organized by the Odysseus Academic Network for Legal Studies on Immigration and Asylum in Europe, the doctoral seminar forms part of the […]

The post Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar & Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
PhD researchers working on European migration and asylum law have a unique opportunity to present their research, receive expert feedback, and potentially receive financial support through the 2026 Odysseus Summer School Doctoral Seminar.

Organized by the Odysseus Academic Network for Legal Studies on Immigration and Asylum in Europe, the doctoral seminar forms part of the prestigious Odysseus Summer School program. It offers selected doctoral candidates the opportunity to engage with leading experts in migration and asylum law while strengthening their research through constructive academic dialogue.

The program is hosted by the Odysseus Network at Université libre de Bruxelles (ULB) and aims to foster collaboration and academic excellence in European migration and asylum policy research.

About the Doctoral Seminar

The doctoral seminar is designed to support PhD researchers by providing a platform to present their thesis projects to a panel of experts drawn from members of the Odysseus Network. Participants receive detailed feedback on their research topic, structure, and methodology.

The seminar is primarily aimed at PhD students in law. However, doctoral researchers from related fields such as political science or international relations are also welcome to apply, provided their research addresses legal aspects of migration or asylum.

Participants benefit from:

  • Direct feedback from renowned academics and specialists in migration and asylum law

  • Advice to strengthen the research design and methodology of their doctoral thesis

  • Networking opportunities with fellow researchers and experts across Europe

  • Exposure to current debates in EU migration and asylum law and policy

Financial Support: Four PhD Grants Available

The Odysseus Network offers four competitive grants to support PhD students attending the summer school and doctoral seminar.

The grant covers:

  • Tuition fees for the summer school (excluding the €350 deposit)

  • Travel expenses

  • Accommodation costs

Please note that daily subsistence and personal expenses are not included.

Grant recipients will be selected based on both their academic record and financial need. Successful applicants will also assist the coordination team with on-site organization during the summer school. This role does not interfere with their participation in the academic sessions.

Eligibility Requirement

Participation in the doctoral seminar is limited to individuals who have already registered for the Odysseus Summer School and paid the required deposit fee.

Applicants seeking financial support must clearly explain their need for funding as part of the application process.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package that includes:

  • Cover page including the applicant’s name, PhD title, year of research progress, and supervisor’s email address

  • Curriculum Vitae with detailed academic background

  • Academic records for each year of study (if applicable)

  • A one-page summary of the PhD research topic explaining its originality and contribution to existing knowledge

  • A one-page overview outlining the structure of the doctoral thesis

  • Proof of payment of the €350 deposit for the Summer School

  • Letter of motivation (required for applicants requesting financial support)

All documents must be merged into a single PDF file before submission.

Application Process

To apply for the doctoral seminar and grants, applicants must:

  1. Complete the registration form for the Odysseus Summer School.

  2. Carefully fill out the PhD catalogue on the Odysseus website.

  3. Submit the full application package by email to: odysseus@ulb.be

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications is 15 May 2026.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early to ensure a complete and competitive submission.

Contact Information

For additional details regarding the doctoral seminar or application process, applicants may contact:

Odysseus Network
Université libre de Bruxelles (ULB)
Email: odysseus@ulb.be

The post Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar & Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
GOAL’s NextGen Youth Programme brings together young people from all over the world to better understand global justice issues and deepen their understanding of Global Citizenship. This programme will guide young, motivated changemakers to learn about the growing crises facing our world with a focus on climate and health systems. Participants will then work together […]

The post GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
GOAL’s NextGen Youth Programme brings together young people from all over the world to better understand global justice issues and deepen their understanding of Global Citizenship.

This programme will guide young, motivated changemakers to learn about the growing crises facing our world with a focus on climate and health systems.

Participants will then work together to imagine solutions for the future by creating awareness-raising materials that will feed into GOAL’s public engagement activities.

When will it take place?

The 2026 Programme will consist of 10 sessions that take place online, with 2 sessions in-person for Irish based participants.

Programme Dates:  

  • Sat May 23 Irish based participants in-person 11-4pm.
  • Tue May 26 International participants online 3-5pm
  • Thu June 4 All participants online 3-5pm
  • Thu June 11 All participants online 3-5pm
  • Saturday- Sunday June 20/21 Irish based participants in person retreat. Time and location TBC.
  • Thu June 25 All participants online 3-5pm
  • Thu July 2 All participants online 3-5pm
  • Thu July 9 All participants online 3-5pm
  • Thu July 16 All participants online 3-5pm

In order to ensure participation of this global cohort, sessions will take place on Thursdays at 3pm Irish Time (8am Honduras, 2pm Sierra Leone, 4pm Zimbabwe, 5pm Ethiopia & Uganda).

What will I learn?

Over these 10 sessions, participants will work with peers globally to develop skills in critical thinking, intercultural communication, project management, teamwork, and the fundamentals of organising and implementing public awareness campaigns.

By taking part you will be empowered to:

  • Understand the diverse and interconnected nature of our world.
  • Critically interrogate the systems that underpin opportunities to engage and flourish in society.
  • Reflect upon your own role and the role of others in tackling local and global challenges.
  • Engage in dialogue with others in a spirit of empathy, inclusivity, solidarity and collaboration.
  • Develop the courage, confidence and resilience to speak out for change.
  • Understand the GOAL’s work and have the opportunity to connect with GOAL staff.

What are we looking for in a candidate?

To be considered, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Current resident of Ireland, Ethiopia, Honduras, Iraq, Sierra Leone, Syria, Turkey, Uganda, or Zimbabwe.
  • Comfortably fluent in English.
  • Between 18 and 25 years old.
  • Available for 10 seminars between May and July. Sessions will fall on Thursdays and Saturdays (see timeline for actual dates).
  • Be open and willing to engage in teamwork in between seminars.
  • Demonstrate basic/emerging understanding of local and global issues.
  • Show clear initiative and desire to be a part of positive change (this could be demonstrated through volunteering, participation in local events, workshop facilitation, tutoring, creative extra-curricular activities, etc.).
  • Interest in participating in artistic methods of learning (this could be demonstrated through a passion for drama, art, creative writing, journalism, music or any other artistic discipline).
  • Reliable access to a “device” and internet is desired but not essential. If you do not have access to a device, please let us know in your application, and we will endeavour to support your participation.

How do I apply?

To apply, please use the fields at the bottom of the page to upload the following:

1.    Your CV or Portfolio
Keep it simple, an outline of your educational background and any voluntary and/or professional experience is sufficient. Alternatively, you may include a short portfolio with examples of your work or projects to date.

2.    A 500 word cover letter addressing the following:

o   Tell us about yourself. Why are you applying for this programme, and why do you believe you are a strong fit?

o   Describe the skills you bring. What strengths or experiences will you contribute, and what do you hope to gain from participating?

Applications who do not follow this format will not be accepted.

                                                                                                          Or

Send us a two- minute video explaining why you are applying for this programme and why you believe you would be a strong fit. Describe the skills and strenghts you can contribute, and what you hope to gain from participating. Video submissions are strongly encourage.

Please note that video submissions need to be sent separately to somalley@goal.ie with your full name and country of participation in the email subject line, alongside your CV or portfolio. The CV/portfolio is a mandatory submission alongside the video application. Applications that do not adhere to these requests will not be considered.

Apply Now

Closing Date

Applications close on April 1st. Successful applicants will be notified of selection by May 2nd. If you are not selected to participate in this year’s cohort, we encourage you to stay in touch with us for other opportunities and to apply again next year.

GOAL’s NextGen Youth Diversity and Inclusion

GOAL is committed to widening participation and achieving equity of access to non-formal Global Citizenship Education. We welcome applications from participants with diverse identities, socio-economically diverse participants, participants with or without disabilities and participants from all ethnic backgrounds.

Stipend Eligibility

In order to support participation, stipends will be available for all successful participants. Stipends are intended to cover costs of internet and data as well as any travel costs incurred during the programme.

Stipends of €400 for the duration of the programme are available for all successful participants.

Apply Now
Source: https://www.goalglobal.org/nextgen-youth-recruitment-2025/

The post GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Welcome to the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, a collaborative effort between UNFCCC’s Youth4Capacity programme and UNDP’s Youth4Climate initiative (powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security) ! Young climate leaders worldwide can now apply for the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, an international youth mentorship initiative designed to equip emerging changemakers with the skills, […]

The post UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, a collaborative effort between UNFCCC’s Youth4Capacity programme and UNDP’s Youth4Climate initiative (powered by the
Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security) !

Young climate leaders worldwide can now apply for the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, an international youth mentorship initiative designed to equip emerging changemakers with the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to drive meaningful climate action.

The programme is a collaborative initiative between the UNFCCC Youth4Capacity Programme and the UNDP Youth4Climate Initiative, powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security. It aims to strengthen youth leadership in climate solutions by providing hands-on training, mentorship, and global networking opportunities.

If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, youth leadership, and environmental innovation, this programme provides an opportunity to learn directly from experts while collaborating with other youth leaders around the world.

About the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme

The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0 brings together a global cohort of youth who are committed to advancing climate solutions in their communities and beyond.

Participants will take part in a structured learning journey that includes interactive bootcamps, peer learning opportunities, and collaborative engagement sessions. The programme is designed to help young leaders build practical skills in climate project development, leadership, advocacy, and social innovation.

By the end of the programme, participants will be better equipped to design impactful climate initiatives, mobilize communities, and contribute to global climate action efforts.

Programme Structure

The programme features three intensive online bootcamps focused on essential skills for climate leadership and project development.

1. Project Design for Climate Action

Participants will learn how to design effective climate projects, including:

  • Developing climate solutions at local and global levels

  • Project planning and implementation strategies

  • Monitoring and evaluating climate initiatives

  • Resource mobilization and fundraising approaches

2. Movement Building and Climate Advocacy

This bootcamp focuses on strengthening youth-led climate movements and advocacy efforts through:

  • Leadership and community organizing

  • Communication strategies for climate advocacy

  • Building inclusive and impactful climate movements

  • Collaboration with stakeholders and partners

3. Entrepreneurial Mindsets for Climate Innovation

Participants will explore how entrepreneurship can drive climate solutions by learning about:

  • Social entrepreneurship for sustainability

  • Innovative climate business models

  • Problem-solving and systems thinking

  • Scaling climate solutions for greater impact

Programme Format

The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme will run for one month and includes:

  • Three online bootcamps

  • 3–4 sessions for each bootcamp

  • Interactive learning with climate experts and practitioners

  • Peer-to-peer engagement through a dedicated online platform

  • Informal networking sessions through Virtual Climate Cafés

This immersive learning structure ensures that participants gain both theoretical knowledge and practical tools for advancing climate action.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants selected for the programme will benefit from a range of learning and networking opportunities, including:

  • Training in climate project design and implementation

  • Skills in leadership, advocacy, and resource mobilization

  • Exposure to global climate experts and practitioners

  • Collaboration with a diverse international youth network

  • Opportunities to exchange ideas through peer-to-peer learning

  • Participation in Virtual Climate Café networking sessions

These experiences are designed to help young leaders strengthen their capacity to develop, lead, and scale climate solutions in their communities.

Opportunity to Attend COP31

Outstanding participants in the programme may have the exclusive opportunity to attend COP31, the United Nations Climate Change Conference.

Selected youth may also be invited to present and pitch their climate project or solution during the conference, gaining global visibility and engagement with international climate leaders and stakeholders.

Certificate of Participation

Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive an official certificate of participation, recognizing their contribution and commitment to climate leadership and youth-led environmental action.

Who Should Apply

The programme is ideal for young climate advocates, sustainability leaders, youth organizers, students, and innovators who want to deepen their impact in climate action.

You should consider applying if you:

  • Are passionate about climate action and environmental sustainability

  • Want to develop skills in project management and leadership

  • Are interested in climate advocacy and movement building

  • Want to collaborate with youth leaders from around the world

  • Have ideas or initiatives focused on climate solutions

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the programme application form HERE. Selected participants will be contacted to join the programme cohort.

This is a unique opportunity to learn from climate experts, collaborate with global youth leaders, and strengthen your capacity to lead climate solutions.

Apply today and become part of a new generation of Climate Catalysts driving global climate action.

For more global youth opportunities, fellowships, scholarships, and leadership programmes, visit:
🌍 www.opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading space research institutions. Applications are currently open for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship Program 2026, designed for high school students who are eager to explore careers in STEM. Hosted […]

The post NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading space research institutions. Applications are currently open for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship Program 2026, designed for high school students who are eager to explore careers in STEM.

Hosted at the Goddard Space Flight Center, the program provides students with a chance to work alongside scientists, engineers, and researchers who are developing cutting-edge space and Earth science technologies.

The internship aims to inspire the next generation of innovators by exposing students to real-world projects, mentorship, and collaborative learning environments within the space science community.

Program Overview

The NASA Goddard Summer Internship offers high school students the opportunity to participate in research-based and technology-focused activities during the summer. Interns will gain valuable exposure to scientific methods, engineering practices, and data-driven problem-solving.

Students selected for the program may work on projects related to space science, Earth observation, astrophysics, computer science, or engineering while learning from experienced professionals at NASA.

Benefits of the Internship

Participants in the program will gain several valuable experiences, including:

  • Hands-on exposure to NASA’s research and technology development

  • Mentorship from experienced scientists, engineers, and researchers

  • Practical experience working on real-world STEM projects

  • Opportunities to explore potential career paths in science and engineering

  • Networking with professionals and fellow students interested in space science

The internship serves as an excellent stepping stone for students who aspire to pursue future studies or careers in STEM fields.

Eligibility

The opportunity is open to high school students aged 16 years or older who will live within 50 miles of the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center during the summer program.

Students who are passionate about science, technology, or engineering are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this unique learning experience.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship 2026 is:

April 17, 2026

Students interested in participating should ensure their applications are completed before the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more about the internship and submit their application through the official link below.

Apply here

For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Visit Here.

The post NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Moving Minds Alliance (MMA) 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis (ECDiC) Research Fellowship Program, an initiative designed to support local researchers working to improve the lives of young children and caregivers affected by crisis. This fellowship aims to strengthen research and evidence on children aged 0–8 years and their […]

The post Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Moving Minds Alliance (MMA) 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis (ECDiC) Research Fellowship Program, an initiative designed to support local researchers working to improve the lives of young children and caregivers affected by crisis.

This fellowship aims to strengthen research and evidence on children aged 0–8 years and their caregivers living in crisis contexts, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. Through financial support and collaborative opportunities, the program empowers researchers to conduct field-relevant studies that influence policies, programs, and interventions supporting vulnerable families.

The Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship seeks to elevate the voices and expertise of local researchers who are closely connected to the communities they study. By supporting locally driven research, the program contributes to building stronger evidence and solutions for children living in challenging environments.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive several forms of support to help them successfully conduct their research and expand its impact.

These benefits include:

  • A one-time stipend of up to $5,000 to support research activities

  • 12 months of tailored mentorship and guidance while carrying out field-relevant studies

  • Opportunities to collaborate with a global network of researchers focused on early childhood development

  • Engagement with the MMA Research Forum team to strengthen research outputs and amplify findings

This combination of financial assistance, mentorship, and collaboration helps fellows produce impactful research that can influence programs supporting young children and families in crisis settings.

Focus Areas

The fellowship supports research related to Early Childhood Development in Crisis contexts, particularly focusing on:

  • Young children aged 0–8 years

  • Caregivers supporting children in fragile or emergency settings

  • Evidence that can inform policy, humanitarian response, and development programs

Researchers are encouraged to design studies that address real-world challenges affecting children and families living in crisis conditions.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 ECDiC Research Fellowship Program is:

March 31, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the program requirements before submitting their applications.

How to Apply

Interested researchers should review the official Terms of Reference and submit their applications through the link below.

Download the Terms of Reference.

For more Fellowship Opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Young Leaders initiative, an opportunity designed to support young changemakers who are working to improve health, wellbeing, and opportunity for young people around the world. Through a series of Impact Fellowships and scholarships to global youth leadership events, this initiative helps emerging leaders strengthen […]

The post Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Young Leaders initiative, an opportunity designed to support young changemakers who are working to improve health, wellbeing, and opportunity for young people around the world. Through a series of Impact Fellowships and scholarships to global youth leadership events, this initiative helps emerging leaders strengthen their work, expand their networks, and amplify their impact.

Open to individuals aged 18 to 35, the Young Health Programme recognizes that young people are already driving innovative solutions to complex social and health challenges. The initiative provides a platform to support these leaders by connecting them with global partners such as One Young World and the AFS Youth Assembly, two well-known international gatherings that bring together youth leaders, policymakers, and experts from around the world.

Participants selected through the Young Health Programme will have opportunities to attend these global events through fully supported fellowships and scholarships, where they can exchange ideas, develop leadership skills, and collaborate with other young changemakers working to create healthier communities.

About the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme

The Young Health Programme (YHP) is a global initiative led by AstraZeneca that focuses on empowering young people to take action on issues affecting youth health and wellbeing. The program works with partners around the world to support youth-led initiatives, strengthen leadership skills, and help young advocates bring their ideas to larger platforms.

Through the Young Leaders initiative, the program identifies passionate individuals who are already making a difference in their communities. Whether they are working on health education, youth empowerment, community wellbeing, environmental sustainability, or social innovation, the program seeks to support those whose work contributes to healthier and more resilient societies.

Selected participants become part of a growing network of youth advocates who are collaborating to improve the future of health for young people globally.

What the Opportunity Offers

Through the Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative, selected applicants can receive support through several pathways designed to help them grow as leaders and expand the impact of their work.

Participants may receive:

Scholarships to attend major global leadership events. These include gatherings such as the One Young World Summit or the AFS Youth Assembly, where youth leaders connect with experts, organizations, and policymakers from across the world.

Leadership development opportunities. Fellows and scholars gain access to training, mentoring, and learning opportunities designed to strengthen their leadership skills and strategic thinking.

Global networking opportunities. Participants join an international community of changemakers working on youth health and social impact initiatives.

Platforms to amplify their work. Through partnerships and global events, selected young leaders have opportunities to showcase their initiatives and gain visibility for the issues they care about.

These experiences are designed to help young changemakers expand their reach, collaborate with others, and scale their solutions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Young people are increasingly at the forefront of solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges. From improving access to health services to promoting mental wellbeing, environmental sustainability, and social inclusion, youth-led initiatives are shaping the future of communities around the globe.

However, many young leaders lack access to the resources, networks, and platforms that could help them scale their work. Opportunities like the Young Health Programme Fellowships and Scholarships help bridge that gap by connecting youth changemakers with global networks, leadership training, and spaces where their ideas can reach a wider audience.

By investing in youth leadership, programs like this not only support individual changemakers but also help strengthen the global movement toward healthier, more equitable societies.

Who Should Apply

The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative is open to young people aged 18 to 35 who are already taking action to create positive change.

Ideal applicants include individuals who:

  • Are leading or contributing to initiatives that improve youth health or wellbeing
  • Are passionate about social impact, community development, or sustainability
  • Want to strengthen their leadership skills and expand their networks
  • Are eager to collaborate with other global changemakers

Applicants may come from a wide variety of backgrounds, including youth organizations, nonprofits, social enterprises, health advocacy groups, or community initiatives.

How to Apply

Applications for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative are now open. Interested applicants should review the eligibility criteria and available fellowship and scholarship pathways on the official program page.

Because different opportunities may have different timelines and requirements, applicants are encouraged to review all available options carefully before submitting their applications.

You can find full details and begin your application on the official AstraZeneca website.

Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website!

The post Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
